title: Notholaena prenticei
taxonomy: Notholaena prenticei is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States, specifically the southwestern deserts. The classification of Notholaena prenticei includes being a member of the genus Notholaena and the order Polypodiales. Further classification places it within the subclass Polypodiidae and the class Polypodiopsida. Additionally, it belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns.

intro: Notholaena prenticei, also known as the Pren's scale fern, is a type of fern native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Notholaena prenticei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
